# apicurio_artifact (Data Source)

The `apicurio_artifact` data source allows you to retrieve metadata and content for an existing artifact in the Apicurio Registry.

## Example Usage

```hcl
data "apicurio_artifact" "example" {
  group_id    = "default"
  artifact_id = "user-schema"
}

output "artifact_content" {
  value = data.apicurio_artifact.example.content
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `artifact_id` (String) The unique ID of the artifact within the group.

### Optional

- `group_id` (String) The ID of the artifact group. If not provided, it defaults to `default`.

### Read-Only

- `content` (String) The actual content of the latest version of the artifact (e.g., the JSON or YAML of a schema or API definition).
- `global_id` (Number) The globally unique ID of the latest version of the artifact.
- `id` (String) The composite ID of the artifact, formatted as `group_id/artifact_id`.
- `state` (String) The state of the latest version of the artifact (e.g., `ENABLED`, `DISABLED`, `DEPRECATED`).
- `type` (String) The type of the artifact (e.g., `AVRO`, `JSON`, `OPENAPI`, `ASYNCAPI`, `GRAPHQL`, `KCONNECT`, `WSDL`, `XSD`, `XML`).
- `version` (String) The version number of the latest version of the artifact.
